methodologies.name: Tools-First, Then RAG
methodologies.summary: Before reaching for retrieval-augmented generation, check whether the knowledge actually needs retrieval. Structured stores want SQL or API calls; small scoped corpora want inlined documents; only genuinely unstructured large corpora want semantic retrieval over embeddings. Naive RAG-first burns engineering effort and produces approximate answers where a typed tool call would have been exact.
methodologies.intent: Force a knowledge-shape check before adopting embedding retrieval — pick the cheapest access mechanism that fits the knowledge.
methodologies.when_to_apply: Starting any knowledge-grounded agent or LLM app — customer-support bot, internal Q&A, docs helper, policy assistant. Apply before the team commits to a vector store. Don't apply once the knowledge has already been shown to be unstructured and high-volume — then RAG is correct from the start.

inputs.name: Knowledge inventory
inputs.description: An enumeration of the knowledge sources the agent must reach — databases, APIs, document repositories, internal wikis, web pages.

inputs.name: Per-source schema or sample
inputs.description: For each source: structure (tables / endpoints / fields), size, change rate, and a handful of representative records.

inputs.name: User query distribution
inputs.description: Sample queries from the target user population — what they actually ask, not what the team imagines they ask.

outputs.name: Access-mechanism assignment per source
outputs.description: Per knowledge source: chosen access mechanism (tool call / inlined doc / RAG / hybrid) and the rationale.

outputs.name: Tool catalogue
outputs.description: The set of typed tool calls (SQL, API, search) the agent will use to reach structured sources.

outputs.name: RAG corpus boundary
outputs.description: Only the genuinely unstructured remainder, scoped tightly.

steps.name: Inventory the knowledge
steps.description: For each knowledge source the agent must reach, record structure, size, and update cadence. Don't proceed until every source is named.

steps.name: Classify each source by access shape
steps.description: Structured store (database, typed API) → tool call. Small scoped corpus (under ~5k tokens of stable text) → inline in the system prompt or load on demand. Large unstructured corpus → candidate for retrieval. Mixed → split the source.

steps.name: Build the tools first
steps.description: For every structured source, author a typed tool: SQL helper, API client, search endpoint. The agent calls these directly; no embeddings involved.

steps.name: Inline the small corpora
steps.description: For small stable corpora, inline them — system prompt, loaded document, scoped context. No retrieval round-trip; full fidelity.

steps.name: Build RAG only for the residual
steps.description: Anything that didn't fit step 3 or 4 — large unstructured text, multi-tenant variable corpora — goes through retrieval. The corpus is now bounded and the retrieval evaluation is scoped.

steps.name: Measure end-to-end correctness
steps.description: On the eval set, compare correctness of tool-call answers against retrieval answers for overlapping queries. If retrieval is winning for a query class that has a structured source, fix the tool, don't tune the retriever.

principles: Knowledge shape determines access shape — pick the mechanism that fits.
Typed tool calls return exact answers; retrieval returns approximate passages — prefer exact when the shape supports it.
RAG is the residual mechanism, not the default.
If a retriever is winning over a tool call, the tool is wrong, not the principle.

known_failure_modes.note: Reaching for RAG as the default mechanism without checking whether the knowledge is structured.

sources.title: Marco Nissen, Working with the models
sources.quote: Don't reach for RAG first. Check whether the knowledge lives in a tool (database, API, search service), a scoped system prompt, or a small inlined document.

sources.title: AI Engineering
sources.quote: RAG Architecture and Retrieval Algorithms ... Agent Overview, Tools, Planning
